Class LingeringEffect

java.lang.Object
logic.effect.LingeringEffect
All Implemented Interfaces:
CombatEffect
Direct Known Subclasses:
BurnEffect, DefenseBuffEffect, DefenseReductionEffect
public abstract class LingeringEffect extends Object implements CombatEffect
An abstract base class representing a combat effect that persists over multiple turns. Unlike instant effects, a lingering effect (such as poison, a stat buff, or a debuff) has a specific duration and can apply recurring logic on each turn, as well as cleanup logic when the effect is finally removed or expires.

  • Constructor Details

    • LingeringEffect

      public LingeringEffect(int duration)
      Constructs a new LingeringEffect with the specified duration.
      Parameters:
      duration - The initial number of turns this effect will last.

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public void apply(BaseUnit target)
      Applies the effect to the target unit. This method acts as the entry point from the CombatEffect interface and delegates the actual application logic to the abstract onInitialApply(BaseUnit) method.
      Specified by:
      apply in interface CombatEffect
      Parameters:
      target - The unit receiving the effect.

    • onInitialApply

      protected abstract void onInitialApply(BaseUnit target)
      Defines the specific logic to execute the very first time this effect is applied to a target. Subclasses must implement this to handle initial stat changes, visual triggers, or immediate damage.
      Parameters:
      target - The unit receiving the effect.

    • tick

      public abstract void tick(BaseUnit target)
      Defines the recurring logic that executes on each subsequent turn or tick while the effect is active. For example, dealing continuous poison damage or draining mana.
      Parameters:
      target - The unit currently affected by this lingering effect.

    • remove

      public abstract void remove(BaseUnit target)
      Defines the cleanup logic to execute when this effect is removed from the target (either because its duration reached zero or it was cleansed/dispelled). Subclasses must implement this to revert stat changes or remove visual effects.
      Parameters:
      target - The unit from which the effect is being removed.

    • reduceDuration

      public void reduceDuration()
      Decrements the remaining duration of this effect by one tick/turn.

    • isExpired

      public boolean isExpired()
      Checks if this effect has completed its lifespan.
      Returns:
      true if the duration is less than or equal to zero; false otherwise.

    • getDuration

      public int getDuration()
      Retrieves the remaining duration of this effect.
      Returns:
      The number of ticks/turns remaining.

    • setDuration

      public void setDuration(int duration)
      Sets the remaining duration of this effect.
      Parameters:
      duration - The number of ticks/turns to set.

    • getSource

      public BaseUnit getSource()
      Retrieves the unit responsible for creating or casting this effect.
      Returns:
      The source BaseUnit.

    • setSource

      public void setSource(BaseUnit source)
      Sets the unit responsible for creating or casting this effect.
      Parameters:
      source - The BaseUnit to set as the originator of this effect.
